 {"id":"bd-3tmk.5","title":"Toast Integration and Demo","description":"Integration with runtime and demo showcase.

## Requirements
- toast() Cmd helper for easy triggering
- Integration with existing demo screens
- Dedicated notification demo section
- E2E tests for notification flows

## Implementation
- Add toast() to ftui-runtime Cmd helpers
- NotificationDemo screen in showcase
- E2E script triggering various notifications

## Success Criteria
- One-line notification from any update()
- Demo shows all notification variants
- No race conditions in queue

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Toast Integration and Demo
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3tmk.6","title":"Toast System E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of notification/toast system behavior.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Toast Flow (toast_basic_e2e.rs)
- Trigger toast, verify appearance
- Auto-dismiss after timeout
- Manual dismiss via click/key
- Multiple toast stacking

### 2. Queue Management (toast_queue_e2e.rs)
- Queue overflow handling
- Priority ordering (urgent first)
- Deduplication of identical messages
- Pause on hover behavior

### 3. Animation (toast_animation_e2e.rs)
- Entrance animation frames
- Exit animation frames
- Animation cancellation on rapid toggle
- Reduced motion preference

### 4. Interaction (toast_interaction_e2e.rs)
- Action button clicks
- Keyboard navigation to toast
- Multiple action buttons
- Dismiss all functionality

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Toast lifecycle events (show, dismiss, action)
- DEBUG: Queue state changes
- TRACE: Animation frame timing
- Metric: Toast display duration histogram

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/toast_system/

## CI Integration
- Snapshot tests at key frames
- Timing assertions (±50ms tolerance)
- Memory leak detection for long sessions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Toast System E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3u5x","title":"Text Effects: Animation Timing Utilities","description":"## Background
Multiple text effects need time-based animation but there's no unified timing system. This causes:
- Inconsistent speed units across effects
- No way to pause/resume animations globally
- No delta-time handling for frame-rate independence

## Implementation

Add AnimationClock struct:
```rust
pub struct AnimationClock {
    time: f64,           // Current time in seconds
    speed: f64,          // Time multiplier (1.0 = normal, 0.0 = paused)
    last_tick: Instant,  // For delta calculation
}

impl AnimationClock {
    pub fn new() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn tick(&mut self) {
        let now = Instant::now();
        let delta = now.duration_since(self.last_tick).as_secs_f64();
        self.time += delta * self.speed;
        self.last_tick = now;
    }
    
    pub fn time(&self) -> f64 { self.time }
    pub fn set_speed(&mut self, speed: f64) { self.speed = speed; }
    pub fn pause(&mut self) { self.speed = 0.0; }
    pub fn resume(&mut self) { self.speed = 1.0; }
    pub fn reset(&mut self) { self.time = 0.0; }
}
```

### Speed Unit Convention:
All effects use **cycles per second**:
- speed: 1.0 = one full cycle per second
- speed: 0.5 = one cycle every 2 seconds
- speed: 2.0 = two cycles per second

Document this in all effect beads.

### Integration with StyledText:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    pub fn time(mut self, time: f64) -> Self {
        self.time = time;
        self
    }
    
    // Or use a clock reference
    pub fn with_clock(mut self, clock: &AnimationClock) -> Self {
        self.time = clock.time();
        self
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_clock_advances: time() increases after tick()
- test_clock_pause_stops: time() frozen when paused
- test_clock_speed_multiplies: speed=2 doubles time advancement
- test_clock_reset_zeros: reset() sets time to 0

## Integration
This bead should be implemented FIRST or in parallel with Easing, as many other effects depend on consistent timing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Animation Timing Utilities
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3ufh","title":"Tick command delivers model updates","description":"Problem: Cmd::Tick currently only triggers redraws. Model::update never sees a tick message, so periodic logic cannot run without external input and tick cadence is unclear.

Scope:
- Define explicit tick semantics (Event::Tick or equivalent) that flows through the same update pipeline as input events.
- Schedule ticks using monotonic Instant; integrate with poll timeout and resize debounce without busy-spin or missed ticks.
- Ensure ticks are disabled cleanly (Duration::ZERO or a dedicated no-tick command) and do not starve input events.
- Document tick behavior and ordering guarantees in runtime docs or comments.

Testing:
- Unit tests in ftui-runtime: tick cadence, coalescing, disable or stop behavior, and no starvation with resize debounce.
- Unit tests covering event ordering when input and tick occur near-simultaneously.
- E2E harness test that logs N ticks, exits deterministically, and produces JSON summary plus PTY capture.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Model receives tick updates reliably and deterministically.
- No busy loop when ticks are disabled or when waiting on input.
- Tests prove cadence, ordering, and disable semantics; docs updated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"TurquoiseCliff","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:26.967603358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:48:55.837816010Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:48:55.837795221Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
